title: Differential Expression Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: vignette demonstrates complete RNA-seq differential expression analysis (DEA) workflow using OmicsKit, study design visualization, using real TCGA-LUAD data (16 tumor vs. 16 normal lung samples). workflow covers: Study design : statistical power estimation Quality control : unsupervised dimensionality reduction DEA results : annotation, normalization, export Visualization : volcano plots gene-level expression plots Multi-comparison : splitting genes exclusive expression cases

title: Differential Expression Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
code: library(OmicsKit) library(ggplot2)

title: Differential Expression Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: collecting data running analysis, good practice estimate minimum sample size needed reliably detect differentially expressed genes given effect size false discovery rate. power_analysis() computes statistical power across range sample sizes using analytical approximation accounts multiple testing:  Statistical power vs. sample size per group. red dashed line marks 80% power; blue dashed line marks minimum required sample size. TCGA-LUAD dataset used vignette 16 samples per group, well minimum required detecting log2FC ≥ 1 80% power.
code: result <- power_analysis(   effect_size  = 1,      # minimum log2 fold-change to detect   dispersion   = 0.1,    # biological coefficient of variation squared   n_genes      = 20000,  # total genes tested   prop_de      = 0.05,   # expected proportion of DE genes   alpha        = 0.05,   # desired FDR   power_target = 0.8,    # desired power (80%)   max_n        = 30,   plot         = TRUE )  # Minimum samples per group needed result$min_sample_size #> [1] 7  # Full power curve head(result$power_table) #>   SampleSize      Power #> 1          2 0.06234486 #> 2          3 0.20477735 #> 3          4 0.41078547 #> 4          5 0.61880322 #> 5          6 0.78217643 #> 6          7 0.88846759  # See plot result$plot

title: Differential Expression Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: running DEA, essential verify samples cluster biological group detect potential batch effects outliers. OmicsKit provides three complementary dimensionality reduction methods accept VST-transformed count matrix. VST? Variance Stabilizing Transformation removes mean-variance dependence RNA-seq counts, placing genes comparable log2-like scale. prevents highly expressed genes dominating sample-level distances.
code: data(vst_counts) data(sampledata)  # nice_PCA, nice_UMAP, and nice_tSNE require a column named "id" sampledata_dim <- sampledata colnames(sampledata_dim)[colnames(sampledata_dim) == "patient_id"] <- "id"  dim(vst_counts) #> [1] 21330    32 table(sampledata$sample_type) #>  #> normal  tumor  #>     16     16

title: Differential Expression Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: PCA fastest method always first step. first two principal components often capture main sources variation. PCA VST-transformed TCGA-LUAD counts. Tumor (red) normal (blue) samples separate cleanly along PC1.
code: nice_PCA(   object       = vst_counts,   annotations  = sampledata_dim,   variables    = c(fill = "sample_type"),   legend_names = c(fill = "Sample Type"),   colors       = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),   shapes       = c(21, 21),   title        = "TCGA-LUAD PCA: Tumor vs Normal" )

title: Differential Expression Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: UMAP captures non-linear structure particularly useful larger datasets PCA show clear separation. UMAP VST-transformed TCGA-LUAD counts.
code: nice_UMAP(   object       = vst_counts,   annotations  = sampledata_dim,   variables    = c(fill = "sample_type"),   legend_names = c(fill = "Sample Type"),   colors       = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),   shapes       = c(21, 21),   title        = "TCGA-LUAD UMAP: Tumor vs Normal",   neighbors    = 5,   epochs       = 1000,   seed         = 174 )

title: Differential Expression Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: tSNE useful visualizing tight local clusters. Note perplexity must less one-third number samples. tSNE VST-transformed TCGA-LUAD counts.
code: # With 32 samples, perplexity must be < 10 nice_tSNE(   object         = vst_counts,   annotations    = sampledata_dim,   perplexity     = 5,   max_iterations = 1000,   variables      = c(fill = "sample_type"),   legend_names   = c(fill = "Sample Type"),   colors         = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),   shapes         = c(21, 21),   title          = "TCGA-LUAD tSNE: Tumor vs Normal",   seed           = 174 )

title: Differential Expression Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: get_annotations() queries Ensembl via biomaRt retrieve gene symbols, biotype, chromosomal location, length. Requires internet connection.
code: annotations <- get_annotations(   ensembl_ids = deseq2_results$gene_id,   mode        = "genes",   version     = "Current",   filename    = "luad_gene_annotations",   format      = "csv" )  head(annotations)

text: add_annotations() joins annotation columns matrix data frame using Ensembl IDs key:
code: # Add gene symbol and biotype to normalized counts norm_counts_annot <- add_annotations(   object    = norm_counts,   reference = annotations,   variables = c("symbol", "biotype") )  head(norm_counts_annot[, c("geneID", "symbol", "biotype")])

title: Differential Expression Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: DESeq2 normalized counts appropriate DEA, TPM useful comparing expression levels genes within sample. Note TPM requires accurate gene lengths, get_annotations() provides via $gene_length (computed end - start + 1).
code: gene_lengths <- annotations$gene_length[   match(rownames(raw_counts), annotations$geneID) ]  tpm_matrix <- tpm(raw_counts, gene_lengths)  # Column sums should all equal 1,000,000 round(colSums(tpm_matrix)[1:3])

text: save_results() exports three Excel files: genes, -expressed , -expressed :
code: save_results(   df           = deseq2_results,   name         = "TCGA_LUAD_TumorVsNormal",   l2fc         = 1,   cutoff_alpha = 0.05 ) # Creates: #   TCGA_LUAD_TumorVsNormal_full.xlsx #   TCGA_LUAD_TumorVsNormal_up_log2FC>1_FDR<0.05.xlsx #   TCGA_LUAD_TumorVsNormal_down_log2FC<1_FDR<0.05.xlsx

title: Differential Expression Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: volcano plot standard way visualize DEA results, showing relationship effect size (log2FC) significance (FDR). Tip: display gene symbols instead Ensembl IDs, run get_annotations() join symbol column deseq2_results add_annotations() calling nice_Volcano(). Volcano plot TCGA-LUAD DEA results. Red: upregulated tumor; blue: downregulated; grey: significant.
code: nice_Volcano(   results   = deseq2_results,   x_var     = "log2FoldChange",   y_var     = "padj",   label_var = "gene_id",   title     = "TCGA-LUAD: Tumor vs Normal",   cutoff_y  = 0.05,   cutoff_x  = 1,   x_range   = 8,   y_max     = 10 )

title: Differential Expression Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: detect_filter() identifies genes reliable expression levels applying thresholds baseMean mean normalized counts per group.
code: # detect_filter requires a column named "ensembl" res <- deseq2_results colnames(res)[colnames(res) == "gene_id"] <- "ensembl" rownames(res) <- res$ensembl  samples_normal <- sampledata$patient_id[sampledata$sample_type == "normal"] samples_tumor  <- sampledata$patient_id[sampledata$sample_type == "tumor"]  detected <- detect_filter(   norm.counts        = as.data.frame(norm_counts),   df.BvsA            = res,   samples.baseline   = samples_normal,   samples.condition1 = samples_tumor,   cutoffs            = c(50, 50, 0) )  length(detected$DetectGenes)

title: Differential Expression Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: get_stars() converts FDR values asterisk notation annotation plots:
code: data(deseq2_results)  res_stars <- deseq2_results colnames(res_stars)[colnames(res_stars) == "gene_id"] <- "ensembl"  # Most significant gene get_stars(   geneID = res_stars$ensembl[1],   object = res_stars ) #> [1] "****"  # Least significant gene get_stars(   geneID = res_stars$ensembl[nrow(res_stars)],   object = res_stars ) #> [1] "ns"

title: Differential Expression Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: nice_VSB() shows distribution normalized expression single gene across sample groups. input normalized counts matrix. Note: alternative using DESeq2 object directly: still DESeqDataSet object session, can use nice_VSB_DESeq2() instead. extracts normalized counts internally via DESeq2::counts(dds, normalized = TRUE), need provide separate norm_counts matrix: Use nice_VSB() working pre-computed normalized counts matrix, nice_VSB_DESeq2() DESeq2 object still available environment. Violin-Scatter-Box plot significant DEG across tumor normal samples.
code: top_gene <- deseq2_results$gene_id[1]  # Get symbol #symbol <- deseq2_results$symbol[1]  nice_VSB(   object      = norm_counts,   annotations = sampledata,   variables   = c(fill = "sample_type"),   genename    = top_gene,   categories  = c("normal", "tumor"),   labels      = c("Normal", "Tumor"),   colors      = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),   shapes      = 21,   markersize  = 3 ) nice_VSB_DESeq2(   object     = dds,   variables  = c(fill = "sample_type"),   genename   = top_gene,   symbol     = "TP53",                 # optional: displayed in plot title   categories = c("normal", "tumor"),   labels     = c("Normal", "Tumor"),   colors     = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),   shapes     = 21,   markersize = 3 )

title: Differential Expression Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: study includes three phenotypes (e.g., normal, primary tumor, metastasis), split_cases() classifies genes 10 mutually exclusive expression patterns based significance direction across three pairwise comparisons.
code: # Requires three DESeq2 result data frames: # df.BvsA : condition 1 vs baseline # df.CvsA : condition 2 vs baseline # df.BvsC : condition 1 vs condition 2  cases <- split_cases(   df.BvsA             = df_tumor_vs_normal,   df.CvsA             = df_meta_vs_normal,   df.BvsC             = df_tumor_vs_meta,   unique_id           = "ensembl",   significance_var    = "padj",   significance_cutoff = 0.05,   change_var          = "log2FoldChange",   change_cutoff       = 1 )  # Number of genes per case sapply(cases, nrow)  # Case 1: ladder genes : progressive up or down across all comparisons head(cases$Case1)

title: Differential Expression Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
code: library(OmicsKit)  # 1. Study design power_analysis(effect_size = 1, dispersion = 0.1, n_genes = 20000)  # 2. QC: dimensionality reduction (use VST counts) data(vst_counts); data(sampledata) sampledata$id <- sampledata$patient_id  nice_PCA(vst_counts, sampledata, variables = c(fill = "sample_type"),          legend_names = c(fill = "Sample Type"),          colors = c("steelblue", "firebrick"), shapes = c(21, 21))  # 3. Annotate and export results annotations <- get_annotations(deseq2_results$gene_id) norm_counts_annot <- add_annotations(norm_counts, annotations) save_results(deseq2_results, name = "TumorVsNormal", l2fc = 1)  # 4. Visualize and filter non-detectable genes nice_Volcano(deseq2_results, x_var = "log2FoldChange",              y_var = "padj", label_var = "gene_id",              title = "Tumor vs Normal")  detected <- detect_filter(as.data.frame(norm_counts), deseq2_results,                           samples.baseline = samples_normal,                           samples.condition1 = samples_tumor)  nice_VSB(norm_counts, sampledata,          variables = c(fill = "sample_type"),          genename = detected$DetectGenes[1],          categories = c("normal", "tumor"),          labels = c("Normal", "Tumor"),          colors = c("steelblue", "firebrick"))  # 5. Multi-comparison (if 3 phenotypes) cases <- split_cases(df.BvsA, df.CvsA, df.BvsC)

title: Pathway Analysis Clustering with OmicsKit
text: gene set enrichment analysis (e.g., CAMERA, GSEA, fgsea), common challenge hundreds significant gene sets returned — many redundant share large overlapping gene memberships. vignette demonstrates use OmicsKit’s pathway clustering functions : Load gene sets GMT files list_gmts() Quantify redundancy gene sets using Jaccard similarity geneset_similarity() Cluster redundant gene sets hierarchically do_clust() Detect communities gene set network generate interpretable labels get_network_communities() Visualize network network_clust() (base R) network_clust_gg() (ggplot2)

title: Pathway Analysis Clustering with OmicsKit
code: library(OmicsKit)  # Suggested packages used in this vignette library(igraph) library(ComplexHeatmap)

title: Pathway Analysis Clustering with OmicsKit
text: real analysis, gene sets stored .gmt files. list_gmts() reads .gmt files directory returns single named list. vignette use built-example data, contains 40 curated gene sets spanning four biological themes: apoptosis, cell cycle, immune response, metabolism.
code: geneset_list <- list_gmts("path/to/your/gmt_folder/") data(geneset_list) data(camera_results)  # How many gene sets? length(geneset_list) #> [1] 40  # What does the enrichment results table look like? head(camera_results) #>                  GeneSet Direction      PValue        FDR #> 1         KEGG_APOPTOSIS        Up 0.017401305 0.04321579 #> 2     HALLMARK_APOPTOSIS        Up 0.012585092 0.04321579 #> 3 GO_INTRINSIC_APOPTOSIS      Down 0.010449980 0.04321579 #> 4 GO_EXTRINSIC_APOPTOSIS        Up 0.007223143 0.04321579 #> 5   HALLMARK_P53_PATHWAY        Up 0.016167120 0.04321579 #> 6     KEGG_P53_SIGNALING        Up 0.006856107 0.04321579  # How many are significant at FDR < 0.05? sum(camera_results$FDR < 0.05) #> [1] 24

title: Pathway Analysis Clustering with OmicsKit
text: geneset_similarity() filters gene sets FDR threshold computes pairwise Jaccard similarity coefficients: J(,B)=|∩B||∪B|J(, B) = \frac{|\cap B|}{|\cup B|} value 1 means two gene sets share identical gene membership; 0 means overlap . $dist_mat slot (1 − Jaccard similarity) can reused independently — example input nice_UMAP() distance-based method.
code: jac <- geneset_similarity(   geneset_list  = geneset_list,   results       = camera_results,   fdr_th        = 0.05 )  # The JaccardResult object contains three slots names(jac) #> [1] "jaccard_sim"  "dist_mat"     "geneset_list"  # Dimensions of the similarity matrix dim(jac$jaccard_sim) #> [1] 24 24 # Preview the top-left corner of the similarity matrix jac$jaccard_sim[1:5, 1:5] #>                        KEGG_APOPTOSIS HALLMARK_APOPTOSIS GO_INTRINSIC_APOPTOSIS #> KEGG_APOPTOSIS             1.00000000         0.43478261             0.39130435 #> HALLMARK_APOPTOSIS         0.43478261         1.00000000             0.52631579 #> GO_INTRINSIC_APOPTOSIS     0.39130435         0.52631579             1.00000000 #> GO_EXTRINSIC_APOPTOSIS     0.31818182         0.08333333             0.04166667 #> HALLMARK_P53_PATHWAY       0.06666667         0.16000000             0.12000000 #>                        GO_EXTRINSIC_APOPTOSIS HALLMARK_P53_PATHWAY #> KEGG_APOPTOSIS                     0.31818182           0.06666667 #> HALLMARK_APOPTOSIS                 0.08333333           0.16000000 #> GO_INTRINSIC_APOPTOSIS             0.04166667           0.12000000 #> GO_EXTRINSIC_APOPTOSIS             1.00000000           0.00000000 #> HALLMARK_P53_PATHWAY               0.00000000           1.00000000

title: Pathway Analysis Clustering with OmicsKit
text: do_clust() performs Ward-D2 hierarchical clustering distance matrix automatically selects optimal number clusters k maximizing average silhouette width.
code: clust <- do_clust(jac)  # Optimal k selected automatically clust$optimal_k #> [1] 10  # Cluster assignments (one row per gene set) head(clust$cluster_assignments) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   NAME                   cluster #>   <chr>                    <int> #> 1 KEGG_APOPTOSIS               1 #> 2 HALLMARK_APOPTOSIS           1 #> 3 GO_INTRINSIC_APOPTOSIS       1 #> 4 GO_EXTRINSIC_APOPTOSIS       1 #> 5 HALLMARK_P53_PATHWAY         2 #> 6 KEGG_P53_SIGNALING           2

text: red dot marks selected k: Average silhouette width vs. number clusters. optimal k highlighted red.
code: clust$silhouette_plot

title: Pathway Analysis Clustering with OmicsKit
text: Gene sets hierarchical cluster appear blocks high similarity (darker blue) along diagonal: Jaccard similarity heatmap Ward-D2 dendrogram.
code: ComplexHeatmap::draw(clust$heatmap)

title: Pathway Analysis Clustering with OmicsKit
text: Hierarchical clustering groups gene sets overall similarity, network community detection captures different structure: densely connected sub-networks within Jaccard graph. get_network_communities() three things single call: Builds binary adjacency matrix (edges Jaccard > threshold) Runs Louvain algorithm detect communities Calls get_superterm() internally generate TF-IDF labels community $superterms$mapping tibble links every gene set community super-term label:
code: net <- get_network_communities(   x             = jac,   threshold     = 0.3,   method        = "louvain",   superterms    = TRUE,   n_terms       = 3,   remove_prefix = TRUE,   seed          = 1905 )  # How many communities were detected? length(unique(net$membership)) #> [1] 16  # Community summary: label + size net$superterms$summary #> # A tibble: 16 × 3 #>    community superterm                     n_genesets #>        <int> <chr>                              <int> #>  1         1 Intrinsic/Extrinsic/Apoptosis          5 #>  2         2 Pathway/Signaling                      2 #>  3         6 Checkpoint/Cell/Cycle                  2 #>  4        14 Glycolysis/Gluconeogenesis             2 #>  5        16 Metabolism/Beta/Oxidation              2 #>  6         3 MITOCHONDRIAL_OUTER_MEMBRANE           1 #>  7         4 CELL_CYCLE                             1 #>  8         5 E2F_TARGETS                            1 #>  9         7 DNA_DAMAGE_RESPONSE                    1 #> 10         8 DNA_REPLICATION                        1 #> 11         9 MYC_TARGETS_V1                         1 #> 12        10 HOMOLOGOUS_RECOMBINATION               1 #> 13        11 NF_KAPPA_B                             1 #> 14        12 INTERFERON_GAMMA_RESPONSE              1 #> 15        13 INTERFERON_ALPHA_RESPONSE              1 #> 16        15 CITRATE_CYCLE_TCA                      1 head(net$superterms$mapping) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   geneset                    community superterm                     #>   <chr>                          <int> <chr>                         #> 1 GO_EXTRINSIC_APOPTOSIS             1 Intrinsic/Extrinsic/Apoptosis #> 2 GO_INTRINSIC_APOPTOSIS             1 Intrinsic/Extrinsic/Apoptosis #> 3 GO_REGULATION_OF_APOPTOSIS         1 Intrinsic/Extrinsic/Apoptosis #> 4 HALLMARK_APOPTOSIS                 1 Intrinsic/Extrinsic/Apoptosis #> 5 KEGG_APOPTOSIS                     1 Intrinsic/Extrinsic/Apoptosis #> 6 HALLMARK_P53_PATHWAY               2 Pathway/Signaling

title: Pathway Analysis Clustering with OmicsKit
text: network_clust() draws plots directly active graphics device returns node attributes invisibly. faster requires igraph. Gene set network — clean view. Node color reflects hierarchical cluster. Gene set network community super-term labels.
code: result <- network_clust(   x                 = jac,   clust_result      = clust,   jaccard_threshold = 0.10,   min_degree        = 1,   superterms        = TRUE,   superterm_data    = net$superterms,   type              = "clean",   seed              = 1905 ) network_clust(   x                 = jac,   clust_result      = clust,   jaccard_threshold = 0.10,   min_degree        = 1,   superterms        = TRUE,   superterm_data    = net$superterms,   type              = "superterms",   seed              = 1905 )

title: Pathway Analysis Clustering with OmicsKit
text: network_clust_gg() returns named list ggplot2 objects can customized, saved ggsave(), composed patchwork. Requires ggraph tidygraph. Gene set network (ggraph) super-term labels. Side--side comparison: clean view (left) vs. super-term labels (right).
code: plots <- network_clust_gg(   x                 = jac,   clust_result      = clust,   jaccard_threshold = 0.10,   min_degree        = 1,   superterms        = TRUE,   superterm_data    = net$superterms,   type              = "all",   seed              = 1905 )  # Display the combined view (super-terms + individual node labels) plots$superterms library(patchwork) plots$clean + plots$superterms

title: Pathway Analysis Clustering with OmicsKit
code: # Save with ggsave ggplot2::ggsave(   filename = "network_combined.pdf",   plot     = plots$combined,   width    = 14,   height   = 14 )  # Save base R igraph plot to PDF pdf("network_superterms_igraph.pdf", width = 14, height = 14) network_clust(jac, clust,               superterms     = TRUE,               superterm_data = net$superterms,               type           = "superterms") dev.off()

title: Pathway Analysis Clustering with OmicsKit
code: library(OmicsKit)  # 1. Load gene sets gsl <- list_gmts("path/to/gmt_folder/")  # 2. Jaccard similarity (filter by FDR < 0.05) jac <- geneset_similarity(gsl, camera_results, fdr_th = 0.05)  # 3. Hierarchical clustering clust <- do_clust(jac) clust$silhouette_plot ComplexHeatmap::draw(clust$heatmap)  # 4. Community detection + super-terms net <- get_network_communities(jac, threshold = 0.3) net$superterms$summary  # 5a. Network plot — base R (draws to device) network_clust(jac, clust,               superterms     = TRUE,               superterm_data = net$superterms)  # 5b. Network plot — ggplot2 (returns objects) plots <- network_clust_gg(jac, clust,                           superterms     = TRUE,                           superterm_data = net$superterms) plots$combined ggplot2::ggsave("network.pdf", plots$combined, width = 14, height = 14)  # 6. Reuse the distance matrix for UMAP nice_UMAP(as.matrix(jac$dist_mat))

title: Pathway Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: vignette demonstrates complete gene set enrichment analysis (GSEA) downstream workflow using OmicsKit, loading gene sets publication-quality visualization. workflow covers: Loading gene sets : reading GMT files list_gmts() Merging results : combining multi-collection GSEA TSV files merge_PA() Gene extraction : retrieving leading edge top-ranked genes getgenesPA() annotating results addgenesPA() Heatmaps : visualizing gene expression per pathway heatmap_PA() (file-path alternative: heatmap_path_PA()) Single-comparison plot : splot_PA() : publication-quality multi-panel barplot one comparison Multi-comparison plot : multiplot_PA() : faceted barplots comparing enrichment across conditions

title: Pathway Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
code: library(OmicsKit) library(ggplot2)

title: Pathway Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: Gene sets used enrichment analysis stored .gmt files, standard format used MSigDB. list_gmts() reads .gmt files directory returns single named list, ready downstream use. vignette, use built-example geneset_list, contains 40 curated gene sets across four biological themes (apoptosis, cell cycle, immune response, metabolism), built-gsea_results dataset, mimics output merge_PA() three comparisons across HALLMARK, KEGG, GO collections.
code: geneset_list <- list_gmts("path/to/your/gmt_folder/")  # Number of gene sets loaded length(geneset_list) # Names of the first five gene sets names(geneset_list)[1:5] # Genes in one specific set geneset_list[["HALLMARK_INTERFERON_GAMMA_RESPONSE"]] data(geneset_list) data(gsea_results) data(deseq2_results) data(vst_counts) data(sampledata) # Ranked gene list: DESeq2 Wald stat from most positive to most negative ranked_genes <- deseq2_results$gene_id[   order(deseq2_results$stat, decreasing = TRUE) ] # Overview of the enrichment results dim(gsea_results) #> [1] 120  15 unique(gsea_results$COMPARISON) #> [1] "TumorVsNormal"      "MetastasisVsNormal" "MetastasisVsTumor" unique(gsea_results$COLLECTION) #> [1] "HALLMARK" "KEGG"     "GO"

title: Pathway Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: real analysis, GSEA produces one results file per MSigDB collection (e.g., H.tsv HALLMARK, C2.tsv KEGG). merge_PA() reads .tsv files directory, standardizes column names, parses leading edge string numeric components (tags, list, signal), computes -log10(FDR), returns single combined data frame. Note: .tsv file must contain Comparison column identifying comparison name (e.g., "TumorVsNormal"). merge_PA() renames COMPARISON. files come single run without column, add manually: built-gsea_results already structure:
code: gsea_data <- merge_PA(   input_directory = "path/to/gsea_results/",   fdr_replace     = 0.001   # replace FDR = 0 (below permutation resolution) ) # Inspect result head(gsea_data[, c("NAME", "NES", "FDR", "COLLECTION", "COMPARISON", "tags")]) your_data$Comparison <- "TumorVsNormal" # Key columns produced by merge_PA() head(gsea_results[, c("NES", "FDR", "Log10FDR",                       "COLLECTION", "COMPARISON", "tags", "SIZE")], n = 2) #>                             NES        FDR  Log10FDR COLLECTION    COMPARISON #> HALLMARK_APOPTOSIS   1.69000339 0.02526288 1.5975172   HALLMARK TumorVsNormal #> HALLMARK_P53_PATHWAY 0.08454987 0.82178574 0.0852414   HALLMARK TumorVsNormal #>                            tags SIZE #> HALLMARK_APOPTOSIS   0.60000000   15 #> HALLMARK_P53_PATHWAY 0.06963633   14

title: Pathway Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: obtaining pathway results, getgenesPA() retrieves gene members relevant enrichment signal. Three extraction modes available: - "le" (GSEA ): leading edge genes : subset drives enrichment score. - "top": top-ranked n % genes rank metric : applicable enrichment method (GSEA, CAMERA, PADOG, etc.). - "": members gene set, ordered rank. addgenesPA() appends gene lists comma-separated columns (le_genes, top_genes, all_genes) pathway results table. Tip: CAMERA enrichment methods produce leading edge information, use genes = c("", "top") set pa_data$top desired fraction (e.g., 0.25 top 25 % rank). request genes = "le" without tags column.
code: # Filter to one comparison pa_single <- gsea_results[gsea_results$COMPARISON == "TumorVsNormal", ] # Optional: define the top fraction for mode "top" pa_single$top <- 0.30   # top 30% of gene set members by rank # Extract genes using all three modes gene_lists <- getgenesPA(   pa_data      = pa_single,   geneset_list = geneset_list,   ranked_genes = ranked_genes,   genes        = c("all", "le", "top") ) # Inspect results for one pathway   ## leading edge   gene_lists$le[["HALLMARK_INTERFERON_GAMMA_RESPONSE"]]    ## top 30% by rank   gene_lists$top[["HALLMARK_INTERFERON_GAMMA_RESPONSE"]]     ## all members   gene_lists$all[["HALLMARK_INTERFERON_GAMMA_RESPONSE"]]   # Append gene columns to the pathway table pa_annot <- addgenesPA(pa_single, gene_lists) # Number of gene sets annotated nrow(pa_annot)  head(pa_annot[, c("NAME", "all_genes", "le_genes", "top_genes")])

title: Pathway Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: heatmap_PA() generates one heatmap per gene set based pa_data_annot. Genes ordered within heatmap position ranked_genes. Output files organized subdirectories format (PDF / JPG) gene selection mode (all_genes, le_genes, top_genes). heatmap shows top-ranked genes HALLMARK_INTERFERON_GAMMA_RESPONSE across tumor normal TCGA-LUAD samples: Expression heatmap top-ranked genes HALLMARK_INTERFERON_GAMMA_RESPONSE across TCGA-LUAD tumor normal samples. Genes ordered DESeq2 Wald statistic.
code: heatmap_PA(   expression_data = vst_counts,   metadata        = sampledata,   pa_data_annot   = pa_annot,   ranked_genes    = ranked_genes,   plot_genes      = c("all_genes", "le_genes", "top_genes"),   sample_col      = "patient_id",   group_col       = "sample_type",   out_dir         = "heatmaps_PA",   pdf             = TRUE,   jpg             = TRUE ) # Creates, for example: #   heatmaps_PA/jpg/top_genes/HALLMARK_INTERFERON_GAMMA_RESPONSE_heatmap.jpg #   heatmaps_PA/pdf/le_genes/HALLMARK_INTERFERON_GAMMA_RESPONSE_heatmap.pdf #   ... (one file per gene set per format per mode)

title: Pathway Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: heatmap_path_PA() convenience wrapper reads inputs disk (expression TSV, metadata XLSX, GMT file, ranked-genes TSV, GSEA TSV) calls heatmap engine internally. useful running analysis immediately GSEA without loading data R. function use? Prefer heatmap_PA() data already R (e.g., calling merge_PA(), getgenesPA(), addgenesPA()). Use heatmap_path_PA() want quick one-call solution reads directly files disk. functions produce identical heatmaps.
code: heatmap_path_PA(   main_dir          = "path/to/analysis/",   expression_file   = "vst_expression.tsv",   metadata_file     = "metadata.xlsx",   gmt_file          = "h.all.v2023.symbols.gmt",   ranked_genes_file = "ranked_genes.tsv",   gsea_file         = "H.tsv",   output_dir        = "leading_edge_heatmaps",   sample_col        = "patient_id",   group_col         = "sample_type",   save_dataframe    = TRUE   # also saves intermediate gene table as .tsv ) # Produces the same output as heatmap_PA() for a single GSEA file

title: Pathway Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: splot_PA() generates publication-quality multi-panel enrichment plot one comparison. Gene sets appear y-axis (grouped MSigDB collection), NES x-axis, −log10(FDR) fill color. Six panels assembled side--side using patchwork. Single-comparison pathway enrichment plot (TumorVsNormal). Gene sets sorted NES; fill color encodes -log10(FDR), capped 2. Collections annotated right. Tip: Use fill_limits = c(0, 2) prevent handful extremely significant gene sets washing color contrast rest. pathway FDR ≤ 0.01 shown maximum color (red).
code: single <- gsea_results[gsea_results$COMPARISON == "TumorVsNormal", ] splot_PA(   data           = single,   geneset_col    = "NAME",   collection_col = "COLLECTION",   nes_col        = "NES",   fdr_col        = "FDR",   order          = "desc",   fill_limits    = c(0, 2),         # cap at -log10(FDR) = 5   fill_palette   = c("white", "red") )

title: Pathway Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
text: multiplot_PA() generates faceted barplot showing NES across multiple comparisons selected set gene sets. facet represents one gene set; bars represent NES per comparison, colored −log10(FDR). layout makes straightforward assess enrichment changes across conditions. Multi-comparison pathway enrichment plot. facet shows NES one HALLMARK gene set across three pairwise comparisons. Fill color encodes -log10(FDR), capped 5. Tip: Use comparison_order control left--right arrangement comparisons x-axis facet, custom_labels replace long comparison names shorter axis labels.
code: # Subset to pathways of interest across all comparisons pathways_of_interest <- c(   "HALLMARK_INTERFERON_GAMMA_RESPONSE",   "HALLMARK_INFLAMMATORY_RESPONSE",   "HALLMARK_G2M_CHECKPOINT",   "HALLMARK_E2F_TARGETS",   "HALLMARK_GLYCOLYSIS",   "HALLMARK_OXIDATIVE_PHOSPHORYLATION" ) multi_data <- gsea_results[gsea_results$NAME %in% pathways_of_interest, ] multiplot_PA(   data             = multi_data,   comparison_col   = "COMPARISON",   facet_col        = "NAME",   axis_y           = "NES",   fdr_col          = "FDR",   comparison_order = c("TumorVsNormal",                        "MetastasisVsNormal",                        "MetastasisVsTumor"),   custom_labels    = c(     TumorVsNormal       = "Tumor",     MetastasisVsNormal  = "Meta",     MetastasisVsTumor   = "Meta/Tumor"   ),   ncol_wrap        = 3,   free_y           = TRUE,   fill_limits      = c(0, 5),   fill_palette     = c("white", "red") )

title: Pathway Analysis Workflow with OmicsKit
code: library(OmicsKit)  #  1. Load gene sets  gsl <- list_gmts("path/to/gmt_folder/")  # 2. Merge GSEA output TSV files  gsea_data <- merge_PA(   input_directory = "path/to/gsea_results/",   fdr_replace     = 0.001 ) # 3. Build ranked gene list (from DESeq2 stat or log2FC)  ranked <- deseq2_results$gene_id[   order(deseq2_results$stat, decreasing = TRUE) ] # 4. Extract leading edge and top-ranked genes  pa_single     <- gsea_data[gsea_data$COMPARISON == "TumorVsNormal", ] pa_single$top <- 0.30 gene_lists <- getgenesPA(pa_single, gsl, ranked, genes = c("all", "le", "top")) pa_annot   <- addgenesPA(pa_single, gene_lists) # 5. Heatmaps  heatmap_PA(   expression_data = vst_counts,   metadata        = sampledata,   pa_data_annot   = pa_annot,   ranked_genes    = ranked,   plot_genes      = c("all_genes", "le_genes", "top_genes"),   sample_col      = "patient_id",   group_col       = "sample_type",   out_dir         = "heatmaps_PA" ) # Alternative: file-path version (reads directly from disk) heatmap_path_PA(   main_dir          = "path/to/analysis/",   expression_file   = "vst_expression.tsv",   metadata_file     = "metadata.xlsx",   gmt_file          = "h.all.v2023.symbols.gmt",   ranked_genes_file = "ranked_genes.tsv",   gsea_file         = "H.tsv",   output_dir        = "leading_edge_heatmaps" ) # 6. Single-comparison enrichment plot splot_PA(   data           = pa_single,   geneset_col    = "NAME",   collection_col = "COLLECTION",   nes_col        = "NES",   fdr_col        = "FDR",   fill_limits    = c(0, 5) ) # 7. Multi-comparison enrichment plot pathways_oi <- c(   "HALLMARK_INTERFERON_GAMMA_RESPONSE",   "HALLMARK_INFLAMMATORY_RESPONSE",   "HALLMARK_G2M_CHECKPOINT" ) multiplot_PA(   data             = gsea_data[gsea_data$NAME %in% pathways_oi, ],   comparison_col   = "COMPARISON",   facet_col        = "NAME",   fdr_col          = "FDR",   comparison_order = c("TumorVsNormal", "MetastasisVsNormal", "MetastasisVsTumor"),   ncol_wrap        = 3 )

what: Add gene columns to pathway analysis results — addgenesPA
title: Add gene columns to pathway analysis results — addgenesPA
text: Appends all_genes, le_genes, /top_genes columns pathway analysis results data frame based output getgenesPA(). Gene symbols within cell comma-separated. Automatically detects column(s) add based structure input.

title: Add gene columns to pathway analysis results — addgenesPA
code: addgenesPA(pa_data, gene_lists)

title: Add gene columns to pathway analysis results — addgenesPA
text: pa_data data frame pathway analysis results containing NAME column. Typically output merge_PA(). gene_lists Output getgenesPA(). Can : list $, $le, /$top slots: multiple modes requested (e.g., getgenesPA(..., genes = c("", "le", "top"))). Adds corresponding columns. flat named list attribute genes_type: single mode requested. Adds corresponding column (all_genes, le_genes, top_genes).

title: Add gene columns to pathway analysis results — addgenesPA
text: input pa_data data frame one additional columns: all_genes: comma-separated string gene set members ordered rank. le_genes: comma-separated string leading edge genes (GSEA ), ordered rank. top_genes: comma-separated string top-ranked genes based user-defined top fraction. Gene sets found gene_lists receive NA.

title: Add gene columns to pathway analysis results — addgenesPA
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(gsea_results) data(geneset_list) data(deseq2_results)  ranked    <- deseq2_results$gene_id[order(deseq2_results$stat,                                           decreasing = TRUE)] pa_single <- gsea_results[gsea_results$COMPARISON == "TumorVsNormal", ] pa_single$top <- 0.30  # Add all three columns gene_lists <- getgenesPA(pa_single, geneset_list, ranked,                          genes = c("all", "le", "top")) pa_annot   <- addgenesPA(pa_single, gene_lists) head(pa_annot[, c("NAME", "all_genes", "le_genes", "top_genes")])  # Add only leading edge genes le_only  <- getgenesPA(pa_single, geneset_list, ranked, genes = "le") pa_annot <- addgenesPA(pa_single, le_only) head(pa_annot[, c("NAME", "le_genes")])  # CAMERA: add only top and all (no leading edge) data(camera_results) camera_pa      <- camera_results colnames(camera_pa)[colnames(camera_pa) == "GeneSet"] <- "NAME" camera_pa$SIZE <- sapply(camera_pa$NAME,                          function(x) length(geneset_list[[x]])) camera_pa$top  <- 0.25 gene_lists_cam <- getgenesPA(camera_pa, geneset_list, ranked,                              genes = c("all", "top")) pa_annot_cam   <- addgenesPA(camera_pa, gene_lists_cam) head(pa_annot_cam[, c("NAME", "all_genes", "top_genes")]) } # }

text: geneset_list named list element character vector gene symbols belonging gene set. Typically output list_gmts(). results data frame least two columns: GeneSet (gene set names) FDR (adjusted p-values). fdr_th Numeric. FDR cutoff retain significant gene sets. Default: 0.05.

title: Compute Jaccard similarity and distance matrices for gene sets — geneset_similarity
text: object class JaccardResult, named list three slots: $jaccard_sim: Numeric matrix pairwise Jaccard similarities. $dist_mat: dist object 1 - Jaccard similarity, suitable clustering UMAP. $geneset_list: Named list gene sets retained FDR filtering.

title: Compute Jaccard similarity and distance matrices for gene sets — geneset_similarity
code: geneset_list <- list(   KEGG_APOPTOSIS      = c("TP53", "BCL2", "CASP3", "BAX"),   KEGG_CELL_CYCLE     = c("CDK2", "CCND1", "TP53", "RB1"),   HALLMARK_HYPOXIA    = c("HIF1A", "VEGFA", "LDHA", "BNIP3"),   HALLMARK_GLYCOLYSIS = c("LDHA", "ENO1", "PKM", "HIF1A") )  results <- data.frame(   GeneSet = names(geneset_list),   FDR     = c(0.01, 0.03, 0.04, 0.20) )  # Only the first three gene sets pass the FDR threshold jac <- geneset_similarity(geneset_list, results, fdr_th = 0.05)  jac$jaccard_sim   # similarity matrix #>                  KEGG_APOPTOSIS KEGG_CELL_CYCLE HALLMARK_HYPOXIA #> KEGG_APOPTOSIS        1.0000000       0.1428571                0 #> KEGG_CELL_CYCLE       0.1428571       1.0000000                0 #> HALLMARK_HYPOXIA      0.0000000       0.0000000                1 jac$dist_mat      # distance object (usable in UMAP, clustering, etc.) #>                  KEGG_APOPTOSIS KEGG_CELL_CYCLE #> KEGG_CELL_CYCLE       0.8571429                 #> HALLMARK_HYPOXIA      1.0000000       1.0000000 jac$geneset_list  # filtered gene sets #> $KEGG_APOPTOSIS #> [1] "TP53"  "BCL2"  "CASP3" "BAX"   #>  #> $KEGG_CELL_CYCLE #> [1] "CDK2"  "CCND1" "TP53"  "RB1"   #>  #> $HALLMARK_HYPOXIA #> [1] "HIF1A" "VEGFA" "LDHA"  "BNIP3" #>

what: Extract gene members from pathway analysis results — getgenesPA
title: Extract gene members from pathway analysis results — getgenesPA
text: gene set pathway analysis results table, retrieves leading edge genes, user-defined top fraction genes, genes gene set, combination. gene lists ordered rank provided ranked gene list.

title: Extract gene members from pathway analysis results — getgenesPA
code: getgenesPA(pa_data, geneset_list, ranked_genes, genes = c("all", "le"))

title: Extract gene members from pathway analysis results — getgenesPA
text: pa_data data frame pathway analysis results. Must always contain: NAME: gene set name. Additionally required depending genes: SIZE: number genes gene set. Required "le" "top". tags: numeric fraction (0-1) genes contributing enrichment score (GSEA leading edge). Produced automatically merge_PA(). Required genes = "le". top: numeric fraction (0-1) defining proportion top-ranked genes extract. Set manually user (e.g., pa_data$top <- 0.25 top 25%). Required genes = "top". Typically output merge_PA(). geneset_list named list gene sets, element character vector gene symbols. Typically output list_gmts(), use built-geneset_list quick testing. ranked_genes character vector gene symbols ordered ranking metric (e.g., DESeq2 stat, log2FC, signal--noise ratio), positive negative. Non-significant genes fall middle list. Used order genes within extracted set. genes Character vector specifying extraction mode(s) use. combination "", "le", "top". Default: c("", "le").

title: Extract gene members from pathway analysis results — getgenesPA
text: Depends genes: Single mode (e.g., genes = "le"): named list element character vector gene symbols. list attribute genes_type used addgenesPA() name output column. Multiple modes (e.g., genes = c("", "le", "top")): named list one element per requested mode: $: named list gene set members. $le: named list leading edge genes (GSEA ). $top: named list top-ranked genes.

title: Extract gene members from pathway analysis results — getgenesPA
text: Three extraction modes: "le": GSEA output . Leading edge genes: subset genes drives enrichment signal. Size computed round(SIZE * tags), tags fraction gene hits (positive ES) (negative ES) peak running enrichment score. Definition GSEA User Guide: "percentage gene hits (positive ES) (negative ES) peak running enrichment score. gives indication percentage genes contributing enrichment score." (https://docs.gsea-msigdb.org/#GSEA/GSEA_User_Guide/). Requires columns SIZE tags pa_data, produced automatically merge_PA(). "top": enrichment result (GSEA, CAMERA, PADOG, etc.). user-defined top fraction genes ordered rank. Size computed round(SIZE * top), top numeric value 0 1 provided top column pa_data. represent true leading edge genes: flexible, rank-based selection suitable exploratory visualization pathway analysis method. Requires columns SIZE top pa_data. "": genes gene set, ordered rank.

title: Extract gene members from pathway analysis results — getgenesPA
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(gsea_results) data(geneset_list) data(deseq2_results)  #or gsl <- list_gmts("path/to/gmt_folder/")  ranked    <- deseq2_results$gene_id[order(deseq2_results$stat,                                           decreasing = TRUE)] pa_single <- gsea_results[gsea_results$COMPARISON == "TumorVsNormal", ]  # ── GSEA results: all three modes available gene_lists <- getgenesPA(pa_single, geneset_list, ranked,                          genes = c("all", "le", "top"))  # But first add the top column (e.g. top 30% of genes by rank) pa_single$top <- 0.30 gene_lists <- getgenesPA(pa_single, geneset_list, ranked,                          genes = c("all", "le", "top"))  gene_lists$le[["KEGG_APOPTOSIS"]]    # leading edge genes gene_lists$top[["KEGG_APOPTOSIS"]]   # top 30% by rank gene_lists$all[["KEGG_APOPTOSIS"]]   # all genes  pa_annot <- addgenesPA(pa_single, gene_lists) head(pa_annot[, c("NAME", "all_genes", "le_genes", "top_genes")])  # ── CAMERA results: use "top" (no leading edge available) ─── data(camera_results) camera_pa      <- camera_results colnames(camera_pa)[colnames(camera_pa) == "GeneSet"] <- "NAME" camera_pa$SIZE <- sapply(camera_pa$NAME,                          function(x) length(geneset_list[[x]])) camera_pa$top  <- 0.25   # top 25% by rank  gene_lists_cam <- getgenesPA(camera_pa, geneset_list, ranked,                              genes = c("all", "top")) pa_annot_cam   <- addgenesPA(camera_pa, gene_lists_cam) head(pa_annot_cam[, c("NAME", "all_genes", "top_genes")]) } # }

what: Detect gene set communities and generate super-term labels — get_network_communities
title: Detect gene set communities and generate super-term labels — get_network_communities
text: Convenience wrapper builds binary adjacency network Jaccard similarity matrix, runs community-detection algorithm, optionally generates super-term labels community via get_superterm(). Designed single step geneset_similarity() network plotting functions network_clust() / network_clust_gg().

title: Detect gene set communities and generate super-term labels — get_network_communities
code: get_network_communities(   x,   threshold = 0.3,   method = "louvain",   superterms = TRUE,   n_terms = 3,   remove_prefix = TRUE,   seed = 174 )

title: Detect gene set communities and generate super-term labels — get_network_communities
text: x JaccardResult object (output geneset_similarity()). threshold Numeric 0 1. Gene set pairs Jaccard similarity value connected network. Default: 0.3. method Character. Community detection algorithm use. One : "louvain" — igraph::cluster_louvain(): fast, recommended use cases. Default. "fast_greedy" — igraph::cluster_fast_greedy(): optimizes modularity greedily, works well mid-size networks. "walktrap" — igraph::cluster_walktrap(): random-walk approach, tends find smaller, tighter communities. superterms Logical. TRUE, calls get_superterm() includes output $superterms. Default: TRUE. n_terms Integer. Number top TF-IDF terms per super-term label. Passed get_superterm(). Default: 3. remove_prefix Logical. Remove database prefix first underscore (e.g., "KEGG_"). Passed get_superterm(). Default: TRUE. seed Integer. Random seed reproducible community detection. Default: 174.

title: Detect gene set communities and generate super-term labels — get_network_communities
text: named list four elements: $communities: igraph communities object. $membership: Named integer vector community IDs, one per gene set. $adjacency_matrix: Binary matrix (1 Jaccard > threshold). $superterms: Output get_superterm() $mapping $summary. NULL superterms = FALSE.

title: Detect gene set communities and generate super-term labels — get_network_communities
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gsl <- list_gmts("path/to/gmt_folder/") res <- read.csv("path/to/results.csv")  # Full workflow jac   <- geneset_similarity(gsl, res, fdr_th = 0.05) clust <- do_clust(jac) net   <- get_network_communities(jac, threshold = 0.3, method = "louvain")  net$membership            # community ID per gene set net$superterms$mapping    # gene set -> superterm net$superterms$summary    # community sizes and labels  # Pass results to network plots plots <- network_clust_gg(   jac,   clust_result   = clust,   superterms     = TRUE,   superterm_data = net$superterms ) plots$combined } # }

what: Generate representative super-term labels for gene set communities — get_superterm
title: Generate representative super-term labels for gene set communities — get_superterm
text: community gene set network, applies TF-IDF (Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency) weighting words present gene set names produce short, representative label called super-term.

title: Generate representative super-term labels for gene set communities — get_superterm
code: get_superterm(   geneset_names,   community_membership,   n_terms = 3,   remove_prefix = TRUE )

title: Generate representative super-term labels for gene set communities — get_superterm
text: geneset_names Character vector gene set names (nodes network). community_membership named numeric integer vector mapping gene set community ID. Typically output igraph::membership() applied community detection result (e.g., igraph::cluster_louvain()). Must length geneset_names. See get_network_communities() simpler workflow. n_terms Integer. Number top TF-IDF terms include label. Default: 3. remove_prefix Logical. TRUE, removes text first underscore gene set names (e.g., strips "KEGG_" prefix "KEGG_GLYCOLYSIS"). Default: TRUE.

title: Generate representative super-term labels for gene set communities — get_superterm
text: named list two elements: $mapping: tibble::tibble() columns geneset, community, superterm — one row per gene set, sorted community. $summary: tibble::tibble() columns community, superterm, n_genesets — one row per community, sorted decreasing size.

title: Generate representative super-term labels for gene set communities — get_superterm
text: TF-IDF works : gene set name treated document word term. TF-IDF upweights words frequent within community rare across communities, making resulting label specific cluster rather generic. frequency-based fallback used TF-IDF returns terms (e.g., small communities). Common pathway words ("pathway", "signaling", "regulation", etc.) standard English stopwords removed scoring. Note: function easily used get_network_communities(), handles community detection calls get_superterm() internally. prefer call directly, need community membership vector:
code: adj        <- (jac$jaccard_sim > 0.3) * 1 g          <- igraph::graph_from_adjacency_matrix(adj, mode = "undirected") comm       <- igraph::cluster_louvain(g) membership <- igraph::membership(comm)  st <- get_superterm(   geneset_names        = names(membership),   community_membership = membership )

title: Generate representative super-term labels for gene set communities — get_superterm
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Recommended: use get_network_communities() which calls this internally net <- get_network_communities(jac, threshold = 0.3) net$superterms$mapping net$superterms$summary  # Direct usage with a pre-computed membership vector adj        <- (jac$jaccard_sim > 0.3) * 1 g          <- igraph::graph_from_adjacency_matrix(adj, mode = "undirected") comm       <- igraph::cluster_louvain(g) membership <- igraph::membership(comm)  st <- get_superterm(   geneset_names        = names(membership),   community_membership = membership,   n_terms              = 3,   remove_prefix        = TRUE )  st$mapping   # per-gene-set labels st$summary   # per-community summary } # }

title: Simulated GSEA pathway analysis results for TCGA-LUAD — gsea_results
text: data frame 120 rows (40 gene sets x 3 comparisons) 15 columns: NAME Character. Gene set name, matching names geneset_list. SIZE Integer. Number genes gene set. ES Numeric. Enrichment score. NES Numeric. Normalized enrichment score. NOM p-val Numeric. Nominal p-value. FDR Numeric. False discovery rate. Approximately 60% gene sets per comparison FDR < 0.05. FWER p-val Numeric. Family-wise error rate. RANK MAX Integer. Gene rank maximum enrichment score. Log10FDR Numeric. -log10(FDR). tags Numeric. Fraction gene set leading edge (0-1). list Numeric. Fraction ranked list used (0-1). signal Numeric. Enrichment signal strength (0-1). LEADING EDGE Character. Leading edge string GSEA format (e.g., "tags=20%, list=35%, signal=15%"). COLLECTION Character. MSigDB collection name: "HALLMARK", "KEGG", "GO". COMPARISON Character. Comparison name: "TumorVsNormal", "MetastasisVsNormal", "MetastasisVsTumor".

title: Plot leading edge heatmaps from pathway analysis results — heatmap_PA
text: expression_data numeric matrix data frame expression values gene symbols Ensembl IDs row names sample IDs column names. Recommended input: VST-transformed counts vst_counts normalized coutns norm_counts. metadata data frame sample annotations. Must contain column matching sample_col (sample IDs) column matching group_col (condition labels, e.g., "Control", "Treatment"). pa_data_annot data frame pathway analysis results enriched gene columns. Must contain column NAME least one all_genes, le_genes, top_genes (comma-separated gene symbols per gene set). Typically output addgenesPA(). ranked_genes character vector gene symbols ordered ranking metric (e.g., stat, log2FC signal--noise ratio), used sort genes within heatmap row. plot_genes Character vector specifying gene columns plot. Subdirectories created automatically based pdf, jpg, plot_genes: <out_dir>/pdf/all_genes/ <out_dir>/pdf/le_genes/ <out_dir>/pdf/top_genes/ <out_dir>/jpg/all_genes/ <out_dir>/jpg/le_genes/ <out_dir>/jpg/top_genes/ Default: "heatmaps_PA". pdf Logical. TRUE, saves PDF heatmaps. Default: TRUE. jpg Logical. TRUE, saves JPG heatmaps. Default: TRUE.

title: Plot leading edge heatmaps from pathway analysis results — heatmap_PA
text: recommended workflow calling function :
code: gsl          <- list_gmts("path/to/gmt/") pa_data      <- merge_PA("path/to/pa_data/") ranked       <- deseq2_results$gene_id[order(deseq2_results$stat,                                              decreasing = TRUE)] gene_lists   <- getgenesPA(pa_data, gsl, ranked, genes = c("all", "le")) pa_annot     <- addgenesPA(pa_data, gene_lists)  heatmap_PA(   expression_data = vst_counts,   metadata        = sampledata,   pa_data_annot   = pa_annot,   ranked_genes    = ranked,   plot_genes      = c("all_genes", "le_genes") )

title: Plot leading edge heatmaps from pathway analysis results — heatmap_PA
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(vst_counts) data(sampledata) data(deseq2_results) data(gsea_results) data(geneset_list)  ranked    <- deseq2_results$gene_id[order(deseq2_results$stat,                                           decreasing = TRUE)]  # ── Example 1: GSEA results (all_genes + le_genes) ──── pa_single  <- gsea_results[gsea_results$COMPARISON == "TumorVsNormal", ] gene_lists <- getgenesPA(pa_single, geneset_list, ranked,                          genes = c("all", "le")) pa_annot   <- addgenesPA(pa_single, gene_lists)  heatmap_PA(   expression_data = vst_counts,   metadata        = sampledata,   pa_data_annot   = pa_annot,   ranked_genes    = ranked,   plot_genes      = c("all_genes", "le_genes"),   sample_col      = "patient_id",   group_col       = "sample_type",   out_dir         = "heatmaps_gsea",   pdf             = TRUE,   jpg             = TRUE ) # Creates: #   heatmaps_gsea/pdf/all_genes/<geneset>_heatmap.pdf #   heatmaps_gsea/pdf/le_genes/<geneset>_heatmap.pdf #   heatmaps_gsea/jpg/all_genes/<geneset>_heatmap.jpg #   heatmaps_gsea/jpg/le_genes/<geneset>_heatmap.jpg  # ── Example 2: CAMERA results (all_genes + top_genes) # camera_results does not contain leading edge information. # Use genes = "top" with a manually set top fraction instead. # Note: top_genes are rank-based and do NOT represent true leading edge genes. data(camera_results) camera_pa      <- camera_results colnames(camera_pa)[colnames(camera_pa) == "GeneSet"] <- "NAME" camera_pa$SIZE <- sapply(camera_pa$NAME,                          function(x) length(geneset_list[[x]])) camera_pa$top  <- 0.25  gene_lists_cam <- getgenesPA(camera_pa, geneset_list, ranked,                              genes = c("all", "top")) pa_annot_cam   <- addgenesPA(camera_pa, gene_lists_cam)  heatmap_PA(   expression_data = vst_counts,   metadata        = sampledata,   pa_data_annot   = pa_annot_cam,   ranked_genes    = ranked,   plot_genes      = c("all_genes", "top_genes"),   sample_col      = "patient_id",   group_col       = "sample_type",   out_dir         = "heatmaps_camera" ) } # }

title: Plot leading edge heatmaps from GSEA analysis results using file paths — heatmap_path_PA
text: main_dir Character NULL. Optional base directory prepended relative file paths. NULL (default), paths used -. expression_file Character. Path tab-delimited expression data file. Rows genes (first column column named NAME used row names), columns sample IDs. Recommended input: VST-transformed counts. metadata_file Character. Path Excel (.xlsx) metadata file. Must contain column matching sample_col (sample IDs) column matching group_col (condition labels, e.g., "Control", "Treatment"). gmt_file Character. Default: "Sample". group_col Name condition/group column metadata file (e.g., "Control" vs "Treatment"). Used heatmap column annotations. Default: "group". save_dataframe Logical. TRUE, saves intermediate data frame (gene sets computed leading edge genes) .tsv file output_dir plotting. Useful inspection reuse. Default: FALSE.

what: Merge GSEA result files into a single data frame — merge_PA
title: Merge GSEA result files into a single data frame — merge_PA
text: Reads .tsv files produced GSEA_merge.sh (GSEA.sh pipeline) directory, standardizes numeric columns, parses leading edge string, computes -log10(FDR), returns single merged data frame ready downstream use splot_PA(), multiplot_PA() , getgenesPA(), heatmap_PA().

title: Merge GSEA result files into a single data frame — merge_PA
code: merge_PA(input_directory, fdr_replace = 0.001)

title: Merge GSEA result files into a single data frame — merge_PA
text: input_directory Character. Path directory containing one GSEA collection result files .tsv format (e.g., output GSEA_merge.sh). file must end .tsv. fdr_replace Numeric. Value used replace FDR q-val = 0. occurs permutation produced NES extreme observed one, meaning true FDR 1 / n_permutations. standard 1,000 permutations, recommended value 0.001. Adjust 1 / n_permutations different number permutations used. Default: 0.001.

title: Merge GSEA result files into a single data frame — merge_PA
text: data frame (gsea_data) containing merged processed GSEA results standardized column names.

title: Merge GSEA result files into a single data frame — merge_PA
text:
Input file format: .tsv file corresponds one MSigDB collection (e.g., H.tsv, C2.tsv) must follow standard GSEA output format following columns: NAME: gene set name. SIZE: number genes gene set. ES: enrichment score. NES: normalized enrichment score. NOM p-val: nominal p-value. FDR q-val: false discovery rate. Values exactly 0 indicate permutation produced equally extreme NES (.e., true FDR permutation resolution 1 / n_permutations). replaced fdr_replace avoid -Inf log-transforms. FWER p-val: family-wise error rate. RANK MAX: gene rank maximum enrichment score. LEADING EDGE: string encoding leading edge statistics format "tags=XX%, list=XX%, signal=XX%". Parsed three numeric columns: tags (fraction gene set leading edge), list (fraction ranked list used), signal (enrichment signal strength). Comparison: name comparison (e.g., "TumorVsNormal"). Renamed COMPARISON output. Required visualization splot_PA() multiplot_PA() . GS
follow link MSigDB GS DETAILS: removed automatically. Output columns: input columns (minus two removed ) plus: COLLECTION: name MSigDB collection, derived file name removing .tsv suffix. tags, list, signal: numeric leading edge components (0-1 scale). Log10FDR: -log10(FDR) computed applying fdr_replace. FDR: renamed FDR q-val. COMPARISON: renamed Comparison.

title: Merge GSEA result files into a single data frame — merge_PA
text: input .tsv files must contain Comparison column identifying comparison (e.g., "TumorVsNormal"). column renamed COMPARISON output required splot_PA() multiplot_PA()  operate multi-comparison mode. files come single comparison column, add manually file merging: your_data$Comparison <- "YourComparisonName".

title: Merge GSEA result files into a single data frame — merge_PA
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Merge all GSEA collection TSV files from a directory gsea_data <- merge_PA(   input_directory = "path/to/gsea_results/",   fdr_replace     = 0.001   # standard for 1000 permutations )  # Inspect result head(gsea_data) colnames(gsea_data)  # Use directly in downstream functions gsl        <- list_gmts("path/to/gmt_folder/") ranked     <- deseq2_results$gene_id[order(deseq2_results$stat,                                            decreasing = TRUE)] gene_lists <- getgenesPA(gsea_data, gsl, ranked) pa_annot   <- addgenesPA(gsea_data, gene_lists)  plot_PA(gsea_data, comparison_col = "COMPARISON") } # }

what: Pathway analysis visualization across multiple comparisons — multiplot_PA
title: Pathway analysis visualization across multiple comparisons — multiplot_PA
text: Generates faceted barplot showing NES values across multiple comparisons set gene sets. facet represents one gene set bars represent NES per comparison, colored -log10(FDR). layout makes easy compare enrichment gene sets changes across conditions (e.g., TumorVsNormal, MetastasisVsNormal).

title: Pathway analysis visualization across multiple comparisons — multiplot_PA
code: multiplot_PA(   data,   comparison_col = "COMPARISON",   facet_col = "NAME",   axis_y = "NES",   fdr_col = "FDR",   comparison_order = NULL,   custom_labels = NULL,   ncol_wrap = 2,   free_y = TRUE,   fill_limits = NULL,   fill_palette = c("white", "red"),   theme_params = list() )

title: Pathway analysis visualization across multiple comparisons — multiplot_PA
text: data data frame pathway analysis results containing two comparisons. Typically output merge_PA(). comparison_col Name column identifying comparison. Appears x-axis facet. Default: "COMPARISON". facet_col Name column used define facets  one facet per unique value. Can original gene set name column (e.g., "NAME") manually curated column cleaner shorter labels (e.g., "clean_name"). Default: "NAME". axis_y Name column use y-axis. Default: "NES". fdr_col Name column containing FDR values. -log10(FDR) computed internally used fill color. Default: "FDR". comparison_order Character vector specifying left--right order comparisons x-axis facet. example, comparison_order = c("BvsA", "CvsA") places BvsA left CvsA right. NULL (default), order follows factor levels comparison_col appear data. custom_labels Named character vector x-axis tick labels. Useful shortening comparison names axis. example, custom_labels = c(TumorVsNormal = "Tumor", MetastasisVsNormal = "Mets"). Default: NULL. ncol_wrap Integer. Number columns facet_wrap. Default: 2. free_y Logical. TRUE, facet uses y-axis scale. Default: TRUE. fill_limits Numeric vector length 2 setting color scale range -log10(FDR). Values outside range clamped nearest limit. example, fill_limits = c(0, 5) maps gene sets -log10(FDR) >= 5 (FDR <= 0.00001) maximum red, value 0 white. Useful one gene set extreme significance makes rest appear uniform. Default: NULL (auto). fill_palette Character vector two colors fill gradient (low high -log10(FDR)). Default: c("white", "red"). theme_params Named list override default theme parameters. See Details.

title: Pathway analysis visualization across multiple comparisons — multiplot_PA
text: ggplot2 object.

title: Pathway analysis visualization across multiple comparisons — multiplot_PA
text: comparisons must combined single data frame column identifying comparison  produced merge_PA(). visualizing single comparison full collection grouping, use splot_PA() instead. theme_params accepts following named elements: bar_col Bar border color. Default: "black". bar_size Bar border linewidth. Default: 0.5. bar_width Bar width. Default: 0.6. hline_size Linewidth horizontal line y = 0. Default: 2. axis_title_size Font size axis titles. Default: 45. axis_text_size_x Font size x-axis labels. Default: 30. axis_text_size_y Font size y-axis labels. Default: 50. tick_size Linewidth axis ticks. Default: 1.5. tick_length Length axis ticks cm. Default: 0.3. strip_text_size Font size facet strip labels. Default: 50. panel_spacing_multi Spacing facets. Default: 0.6.

title: Pathway analysis visualization across multiple comparisons — multiplot_PA
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gsea_results <- merge_PA("path/to/gsea_results/")  # Basic multi-comparison plot multiplot_PA(   data           = gsea_results,   comparison_col = "COMPARISON",   facet_col      = "NAME",   fdr_col        = "FDR",   ncol_wrap      = 3 )  # Control left-to-right order of comparisons on the x-axis multiplot_PA(   data             = gsea_results,   comparison_col   = "COMPARISON",   facet_col        = "NAME",   fdr_col          = "FDR",   comparison_order = c("BvsA", "CvsA")   # BvsA on the left, CvsA on the right )  # Use cleaner facet labels and shorten x-axis tick names gsea_results$clean_name <- gsub("_", " ", gsea_results$NAME)  multiplot_PA(   data             = gsea_results,   comparison_col   = "COMPARISON",   facet_col        = "clean_name",   fdr_col          = "FDR",   comparison_order = c("BvsA", "CvsA"),   custom_labels    = c(BvsA = "Tumor", CvsA = "Metastasis") ) } # }

title: Gene set network clustering with igraph base R graphics — network_clust

title: Gene set network clustering with igraph base R graphics — network_clust

title: Gene set network clustering with igraph base R graphics — network_clust
text: x JaccardResult object (output geneset_similarity()). clust_result list returned do_clust(), used color nodes hierarchical cluster assignment. jaccard_threshold Numeric. Minimum Jaccard similarity required edge included network. Default: 0.50. min_degree Integer. Minimum node degree node retained network. Default: 2. superterms Logical. TRUE, overlays super-term labels community centroids relevant plots. Requires superterm_data. Default: FALSE. superterm_data list returned get_superterm(). Required superterms = TRUE. type Character. plot(s) draw. One : "clean" : colored nodes, labels. "superterms" : colored nodes community super-term labels. "combined" : colored nodes super-term labels individual node labels. "individual" : colored nodes individual node labels . "" : four plots drawn sequentially. Default. seed Integer. Random seed Fruchterman-Reingold layout Louvain community detection. Default: 174.

title: Gene set network clustering with igraph base R graphics — network_clust
text: Invisibly, named list : $graph: filtered igraph graph object (g_clean). $layout: Numeric matrix normalized Fruchterman-Reingold node coordinates. $node_attributes: tibble::tibble() columns NAME, cluster, community, degree, betweenness, closeness (plus superterm superterms = TRUE). $superterm_report: tibble::tibble() columns community, superterm, n_genesets, geneset_members. present superterms = TRUE.

title: Gene set network clustering with igraph base R graphics — network_clust

title: Gene set network clustering with igraph base R graphics — network_clust
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Requires igraph gsl <- list_gmts("path/to/gmt_folder/") res <- read.csv("path/to/results.csv")  jac   <- geneset_similarity(gsl, res, fdr_th = 0.05) clust <- do_clust(jac) net   <- get_network_communities(jac, threshold = 0.3)  # Draw all four plots directly to the active graphics device result <- network_clust(   jac,   clust_result   = clust,   superterms     = TRUE,   superterm_data = net$superterms,   type           = "all",   seed           = 174 )  # Draw only the clean version (no labels) network_clust(jac, clust, type = "clean")  # Access node-level metrics from the invisible return result$node_attributes result$superterm_report  # Save a specific plot to PDF pdf("network_superterms.pdf", width = 14, height = 14) network_clust(jac, clust, superterms = TRUE,               superterm_data = net$superterms, type = "superterms") dev.off() } # }

text: data Data.frame. Must contain columns specified gene, time_var, event_var. gene String. Name column data indicating mutation status (values ''/'Yes'). time_var String. Name column survival time. event_var String. Name column event indicator (0/1). coord Numeric NULL. X-coordinate display log-rank p-value. NULL, placed midpoint time range. title_prefix String. title: Obtain 10 exclusive cases from 3 comparisons. — split_cases
text: named list 10 data frames ($Case1 $Case10), containing genes belonging mutually exclusive expression pattern. Cases 1–6 contain trend column ("" "dn"). Case 10 contains genes significant comparison.

title: Obtain 10 exclusive cases from 3 comparisons. — split_cases
text: 10 cases : Case 1 : Ladder: significant 3, direction. Case 2 : Stronger condition 1: significant 3, direction reverses conditions. Case 3 : Stronger condition 2. Case 4 : Marker condition 2: significant CvsA BvsC . Case 5 : Marker condition 1: significant BvsA BvsC . Case 6 : Shared: significant BvsA CvsA . Cases 7-9 : Significant one comparison. Case 10 : significant comparison.

title: Obtain 10 exclusive cases from 3 comparisons. — split_cases
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # split_cases requires three DESeq2 comparisons. # Simulate a 3-phenotype study: Normal (A), Primary (B), Metastasis (C) set.seed(174) n_genes <- 500  make_res <- function(seed) {   set.seed(seed)   data.frame(     ensembl        = paste0("ENSG", sprintf("%011d", seq_len(n_genes))),     log2FoldChange = rnorm(n_genes, 0, 1.5),     padj           = runif(n_genes, 0, 0.5),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) }  df_BvsA <- make_res(1) df_CvsA <- make_res(2) df_BvsC <- make_res(3)  cases <- split_cases(   df.BvsA              = df_BvsA,   df.CvsA              = df_CvsA,   df.BvsC              = df_BvsC,   unique_id            = "ensembl",   significance_var     = "padj",   significance_cutoff  = 0.25,   change_var           = "log2FoldChange",   change_cutoff        = 0 )  # Number of genes per case sapply(cases, nrow)  # Inspect Case 1 (ladder genes: significant in all 3 comparisons) head(cases$Case1) } # }

what: Pathway analysis visualization for a single comparison — splot_PA
title: Pathway analysis visualization for a single comparison — splot_PA
text: Generates publication-quality multi-panel pathway enrichment plot single comparison using patchwork. Gene sets appear y-axis grouped MSigDB collection, NES x-axis, -log10(FDR) fill color. Six panels assembled side side: "Pathways" label, gene set names, NES bar chart, collection labels, "MSigDB" label, color legend.

title: Pathway analysis visualization for a single comparison — splot_PA
code: splot_PA(   data,   geneset_col = "NAME",   collection_col = "COLLECTION",   nes_col = "NES",   fdr_col = "FDR",   order = "desc",   fill_limits = NULL,   fill_palette = c("white", "red"),   theme_params = list() )

title: Pathway analysis visualization for a single comparison — splot_PA
text: data data frame pathway analysis results single comparison. Typically output merge_PA() filtered one value COMPARISON column, results single CAMERA/GSEA run. Must contain columns specified geneset_col, collection_col, nes_col, fdr_col. geneset_col Name column containing gene set labels shown y-axis. Default: "NAME". collection_col Name column containing MSigDB collection labels used group gene sets (e.g., "KEGG", "HALLMARK", "GO"). Default: "COLLECTION". nes_col Name column containing NES values (x-axis). Default: "NES". fdr_col Name column containing FDR values. -log10(FDR) computed internally used fill color. Default: "FDR". order One "desc" "asc". Sort order NES values y-axis. Default: "desc". fill_limits Numeric vector length 2 setting color scale range -log10(FDR). Values outside range clamped nearest limit. example, fill_limits = c(0, 5) maps gene sets -log10(FDR) >= 5 (.e., FDR <= 0.00001) maximum color (red), value 0 minimum color (white). Useful gene sets extreme significance washes color variation rest. Default: NULL (auto  uses actual data range). fill_palette Character vector two colors fill gradient (low high -log10(FDR)). Default: c("white", "red"). theme_params Named list override default theme parameters. See Details.

title: Pathway analysis visualization for a single comparison — splot_PA
text: patchwork object combining six ggplot2 panels.

title: Pathway analysis visualization for a single comparison — splot_PA
text: visualizing enrichment across multiple comparisons, use multiplot_PA() instead. theme_params accepts following named elements: side_label_size Size "Pathways" "MSigDB" labels. Default: 35. geneset_text_size Text size gene set labels. Default: 5. collection_text_size Text size collection labels. Default: 5. panel_widths Patchwork relative widths 6 panels. Default: c(4, 25, 15, 3, 10, 3). col_size Border linewidth geom_col. Default: 1. axis_title_size Font size axis titles. Default: 45. axis_text_size_x Font size x-axis labels. Default: 30. tick_size Linewidth axis ticks. Default: 1.5. tick_length Length axis ticks cm. Default: 0.3. panel_spacing_single Spacing facets. Default: 4.

title: Pathway analysis visualization for a single comparison — splot_PA
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gsea_results <- merge_PA("path/to/gsea_results/")  # Filter to one comparison single <- gsea_results[gsea_results$COMPARISON == "TumorVsNormal", ]  splot_PA(   data           = single,   geneset_col    = "NAME",   collection_col = "COLLECTION",   nes_col        = "NES",   fdr_col        = "FDR" )  # Cap color scale at -log10(FDR) = 5 so subtle differences are visible # (gene sets with FDR <= 0.00001 all get the same max red color) splot_PA(   data        = single,   geneset_col = "NAME", collection_col = "COLLECTION",   nes_col     = "NES",  fdr_col        = "FDR",   fill_limits = c(0, 5) ) } # }

title: Function to calculate the TPMs from a table of raw gene counts. — tpm
text: TPM: Transcript per million. See https://www.biostars.org/p/273537/ input table numeric: row names gene identifiers (ensembl ID). column names represent samples. gene lengths column dataframe row order.

title: Function to calculate the TPMs from a table of raw gene counts. — tpm
code: tpm(raw_counts, gene_lengths)

title: Function to calculate the TPMs from a table of raw gene counts. — tpm
text: raw_counts table gene counts. gene_lengths column gene lengths.

title: Function to calculate the TPMs from a table of raw gene counts. — tpm
text: numeric matrix dimensions raw_counts TPM values. Column sums equal 1,000,000 definition.

title: Function to calculate the TPMs from a table of raw gene counts. — tpm
text: TPM normalizes sequencing depth gene length, making values comparable genes within sample. appropriate differential expression analysis, use DESeq2 normalized counts (norm_counts) purpose. Gene lengths get_annotations() genomic lengths (including introns); higher accuracy use transcript-level lengths.

title: Function to calculate the TPMs from a table of raw gene counts. — tpm
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(raw_counts) data(deseq2_results)  # Gene lengths are needed, retrieve from get_annotations() or use # pre-fetched lengths. Here we use the gene_length column if available. annotations <- get_annotations(   ensembl_ids = rownames(raw_counts),   mode        = "genes" )  # Match gene lengths to raw_counts row order gene_lengths <- annotations$gene_length[   match(rownames(raw_counts), annotations$geneID) ]  # Calculate TPM tpm_matrix <- tpm(raw_counts, gene_lengths)  # Check: column sums should all be 1,000,000 round(colSums(tpm_matrix)[1:3]) } # }

what: Variance-stabilized counts matrix for TCGA-LUAD — vst_counts
title: Variance-stabilized counts matrix for TCGA-LUAD — vst_counts
text: Variance Stabilizing Transformation (VST) applied TCGA-LUAD RNA-seq dataset (16 tumor samples, 16 normal samples) using DESeq2::vst() blind = TRUE. VST removes mean-variance dependence characteristic RNA-seq count data, placing genes comparable log2-like scale. makes appropriate input sample-level dimensionality reduction clustering methods.

title: Variance-stabilized counts matrix for TCGA-LUAD — vst_counts
code: vst_counts

title: Variance-stabilized counts matrix for TCGA-LUAD — vst_counts
text: numeric matrix 21,330 rows (genes) 32 columns (samples): rows Ensembl gene IDs (e.g., "ENSG00000141510"). columns Sample IDs matching patient_id column sampledata. values Numeric. VST-transformed expression values log2-like scale. Range: [1.78, 20.85].

title: Variance-stabilized counts matrix for TCGA-LUAD — vst_counts
text: TCGA-LUAD STAR counts downloaded GDC Data Portal (https://gdc-hub.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/download/TCGA-LUAD.star_counts.tsv.gz). Transformed DESeq2::vst() (blind = TRUE); generated data-raw/deseq2_results.R.

title: Variance-stabilized counts matrix for TCGA-LUAD — vst_counts
text: Suitable input nice_PCA(), nice_UMAP(), nice_tSNE(). gene-level expression plots (nice_VSB()) filtering (detect_filter()) use norm_counts instead.

title: Variance-stabilized counts matrix for TCGA-LUAD — vst_counts
code: data(vst_counts) data(sampledata)  # Dimensions dim(vst_counts) #> [1] 21330    32  # Value range (log2-like scale) range(vst_counts) #> [1]  1.784263 20.849634  # PCA plot colored by sample type colnames(sampledata)[colnames(sampledata) == "patient_id"] <- "id" nice_PCA(   object      = vst_counts,   annotations = sampledata,   variables   = c(fill = "sample_type"),   legend_names = c(fill = "Sample Type"),   colors      = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),   shapes      = c(21, 21),   title       = "TCGA-LUAD PCA" )   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # UMAP plot colnames(sampledata)[colnames(sampledata) == "patient_id"] <- "id"  nice_UMAP(   object      = vst_counts,   annotations = sampledata,   variables   = c(fill = "sample_type"),   legend_names = c(fill = "Sample Type"),   colors      = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),   shapes      = c(21, 21),   title       = "TCGA-LUAD UMAP" )  # tSNE plot # perplexity must be lower than the number of samples divided by 3  colnames(sampledata)[colnames(sampledata) == "patient_id"] <- "id" nice_tSNE(   object       = vst_counts,   annotations  = sampledata,   perplexity   = 5,   variables    = c(fill = "sample_type"),   legend_names = c(fill = "Sample Type"),   colors       = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),   shapes       = c(21, 21),   title        = "TCGA-LUAD tSNE" ) } # }
